title: AT A DINNER PARTY
conversation: Delayed by traffic, Lori and Mike arrive late to a dinner party. Their hosts, Lisa and Todd, tell them not to worry and then take their drink order. Lori: I'm sorry we're late. We got held up in traffic on Route 95. Mike: There was a pile-up on the highway involving three cars. A deer ran across the highway. Lori: We were sitting in traffic for an hour. We would've been better off walking here! Lisa: What a nightmare! Come on in and chill out now. Join the party! Lori: (handing Lisa a bottle of wine) This is for you and Todd. Lisa: Thank you. Lori: (handing Lisa a toy car) And this is a little something for your son. Lisa: Oh, how kind of you! You shouldn't have. Lori: It's our pleasure. Todd: Can I get you a drink? Mike: A drink would hit the spot. What do you have? Todd: You name it, we've got it. Lori: I'll take a Bloody Mary.* Mike: Lori, if you took Tylenol for your toothache, you'd be better off not drinking any alcohol. Lori: Make that a virgin ** Bloody Mary. Hold the vodka. Todd: And what can I get you, Mike? Mike: I'll take a vodka on the rocks. Todd: Coming right up!

idioms.example: I'm sorry I'm late; I was held up at the office by a last-minute meeting.
idioms.context: We got held up in traffic on Route 95.

idioms.meaning_en: a traffic accident involving several vehicles
idioms.meaning_vi: vụ tai nạn liên hoàn (nhiều xe đâm nhau)
idioms.example: The heavy fog caused a 10-car pile-up on the bridge.
idioms.context: There was a pile-up on the highway involving three cars.

idioms.meaning_en: to be stuck in a line of vehicles that are not moving or moving very slowly
idioms.meaning_vi: kẹt xe, chôn chân trong đám tắc đường
idioms.example: I hate sitting in traffic when I'm already late for an appointment.
idioms.context: We were sitting in traffic for an hour.

idioms.meaning_en: used to describe a very unpleasant or difficult situation
idioms.meaning_vi: Thật là một cơn ác mộng/thảm họa!
idioms.example: The hotel lost our reservation and it started raining; what a nightmare!
idioms.context: What a nightmare! Come on in and chill out now.

idioms.example: We spent the whole weekend just chilling out by the pool.
idioms.context: Come on in and chill out now.

idioms.meaning_en: an invitation to participate in the social gathering or activity
idioms.meaning_vi: Cùng tham gia bữa tiệc nào!
idioms.example: Don't just stand in the hallway, come inside and join the party!

idioms.meaning_en: a small gift given to someone
idioms.meaning_vi: một món quà nhỏ, chút quà mọn
idioms.example: I brought a little something to say thank you for your help.
idioms.context: And this is a little something for your son.

idioms.meaning_en: a polite way to thank someone for a gift, implying it was too generous
idioms.meaning_vi: Bạn không cần phải làm thế đâu (lời cảm ơn lịch sự khi nhận quà)
idioms.example: Flowers? Oh, you shouldn't have, but they are beautiful!
idioms.context: Oh, how kind of you! You shouldn't have.

idioms.meaning_en: to be exactly what is needed, especially food or drink
idioms.meaning_vi: thật đúng lúc, giải tỏa cơn khát/đói
idioms.example: That cold lemonade really hit the spot after our long walk.
idioms.context: A drink would hit the spot.

idioms.idiom: You name it, we've got it.
idioms.meaning_en: everything you can think of is available
idioms.meaning_vi: bất cứ cái gì bạn muốn, chúng tôi đều có
idioms.example: Our library has books, movies, games—you name it, we've got it.
idioms.context: You name it, we've got it.

idioms.meaning_en: to leave something out of a food or drink order
idioms.example: I'll have a cheeseburger, but hold the pickles, please.

idioms.meaning_en: a drink served with ice cubes
idioms.example: I'll have a Scotch on the rocks, please.
idioms.context: I'll take a vodka on the rocks.

idioms.meaning_en: the order will be served or finished very quickly
idioms.example: Two cheeseburgers and a large soda? Coming right up!
